faqs.question: What are the most common pantry pests in Clifton homes?
faqs.answer: The most common pantry pests in Clifton include Indian meal moths, flour beetles, cigarette beetles, and grain weevils. These pests infest dry goods like cereals, flour, pasta, spices, and pet food. Our technicians thoroughly inspect all food storage areas to identify the source and eliminate the infestation completely.

faqs.question: How much does pantry pest control cost in Clifton?
faqs.answer: Pantry pest control services in Clifton range from $350 to $550, depending on the severity of the infestation, the size of your home, and the number of treatment areas required. We provide free inspections and detailed quotes before beginning any work, with no hidden fees.

faqs.question: How quickly can you respond to pantry pest problems in Clifton?
faqs.answer: We offer same-day and next-day service to Clifton residents dealing with pantry pest infestations. Our team understands that contaminated food is both a health concern and a financial loss, so we prioritize rapid response times. Simply call us in the morning, and we can often arrive the same afternoon.

faqs.question: Do I need to throw away all my pantry food after treatment?
faqs.answer: Not necessarily all of it. During our inspection, we'll identify which food items are infested and must be discarded. Sealed, unopened packages in unaffected areas may be safe to keep. We'll provide specific guidance on what to keep and what to discard, plus prevention tips to protect your food storage going forward, including proper container recommendations.
